products.name: Enterprise CPMS

interfaces.name: Web Portal
interfaces.description: Primary operations workspace for charge point onboarding, monitoring, remote actions, and network administration.

interfaces.name: Public API
interfaces.description: Controlled API surface for site, charger, availability, and interoperability data exchange with external systems.

interfaces.name: Partner Integrations
interfaces.description: Integration interfaces for roaming hubs, enterprise systems, and acquired backend landscapes.

neededCapabilities.whyNeeded: Connects heterogeneous chargers and supports remote operations during network expansion.
Provides the core operational tooling for configuration, fleet-wide management, and commissioning.
Directly supports uptime, incident detection, and remote remediation targets.
Coordinates charging sessions across stations, users, and backend processes.
Enables load management and energy optimization required by large CPO operations.
Captures site-level power constraints needed for safe and efficient smart charging decisions.
Supports roaming access and interoperability with external eMSP networks.
Exposes stable external interfaces and secures enterprise and partner integrations.
Streams charger and session telemetry for operational reactions and downstream analytics.
Stores operational history and performance data needed for SLA and network reporting.
Supports platform reliability, alerting, and enterprise-grade operational control.
Covers regulatory compliance, auditability, and secure operation of the charging backend.

products.neededCapabilitiesMappingNote: Capabilities are inferred by aligning CPO jobs, product strategy, and the available eMobility brick catalog.

products.name: Roaming & Settlement Hub

interfaces.name: Public API
interfaces.description: External API surface for roaming connectivity, authorization, and transaction exchange.

interfaces.name: Partner Portal
interfaces.description: Operational portal for partner onboarding, issue handling, and settlement transparency.

interfaces.name: API
interfaces.description: Machine-to-machine interfaces for session authorization, CDR exchange, and dispute workflows.

neededCapabilities.whyNeeded: Acts as the core interoperability layer across roaming participants.
Supports onboarding, credential management, and commercial agreements with roaming partners.
Processes CDRs, settlement events, and operational disputes across networks.
Provides the low-latency authorization flow required for successful roaming sessions.
Supports transparent cross-border pricing and commercial rule management.
Provides the financial event backbone required for rating and reconciliation.
Enables automated invoicing and settlement between roaming counterparties.
Exposes partner-facing APIs with governance, throttling, and secure access.
Connects the roaming hub to finance, CRM, and external partner systems.
Tracks partner relationships, customer data, and the contractual basis for roaming services.

products.neededCapabilitiesMappingNote: Capabilities are inferred from eMSP customer needs around roaming reliability, authorization, and settlement automation.

products.name: EV Driver Experience

interfaces.name: iOS
interfaces.description: Native iOS app for charger discovery, session control, pricing transparency, and receipts.

interfaces.name: Android
interfaces.description: Native Android app for locating chargers, starting sessions, and managing charging history.

interfaces.name: Web App
interfaces.description: Browser-based experience for account access, receipts, support, and trip planning.

neededCapabilities.whyNeeded: Provides the primary customer-facing experience for discovery, charging, and account management.
Publishes charger availability and location data to the driver surfaces.
Supports fast, reliable session authentication from app or card.
Enables clear up-front pricing and tariff transparency before session start.
Maintains the rated charging record behind accurate invoices and session history.
Supports payment, digital receipts, and billing accuracy for drivers.
Expands charger access across third-party networks and countries.
Maintains driver accounts, tokens, and charging entitlements across channels.
Measures session success, pricing issues, and usage patterns to improve the consumer experience.

products.neededCapabilitiesMappingNote: Capabilities are inferred from EV driver jobs around charger discovery, session start reliability, and billing transparency.

products.name: Fleet Charging & Energy Manager

interfaces.name: Web Portal
interfaces.description: Primary fleet workspace for access control, charging policy management, reimbursement, and cost analytics.

interfaces.name: Public API
interfaces.description: External APIs for fleet data exchange, policy integration, and cross-system reporting.

interfaces.name: ERP Integrations
interfaces.description: Enterprise integration interfaces for expense, HR, accounting, and reimbursement systems.

neededCapabilities.whyNeeded: Provides fleet-level driver, vehicle, and policy management workflows.
Maintains fleet accounts, contracts, charge cards, and driver lifecycle data.
Supports tariff controls, driver charging policies, and cost governance.
Enables consolidated billing and automated reimbursement flows across charging contexts.
Provides the fleet reporting and cost transparency needed for TCO and ESG management.
Integrates fleet charging data with ERP, HR, and expense systems.
Applies fleet charging entitlements and policies during session authorization.
Supports optimization of charging schedules and site energy costs for fleets.
Models site constraints for depot and workplace charging optimization.
Stores detailed fleet charging history needed for reimbursement, analytics, and audits.

products.neededCapabilitiesMappingNote: Capabilities are inferred from fleet-management jobs focused on access control, reporting, reimbursement, and energy optimization.

interfaces.name: Multi-tenant Identity Service
interfaces.description: Shared identity, account, and authorization services across operator, fleet, partner, and driver products.

interfaces.name: Compliance & Audit Controls
interfaces.description: Common controls for secure communications, auditability, and regulatory readiness.

interfaces.name: Telemetry and Event Backbone
interfaces.description: Shared eventing and telemetry flow across charge points, sessions, and downstream analytics.

interfaces.name: Enterprise Integration Services
interfaces.description: Canonical integration services connecting the platform to ERP, CRM, payment, and partner ecosystems.

enrichmentMetadata.note: The emobility product portfolio is derived by grouping the capability catalog into products aligned with customer jobs, interfaces, and strategy horizons.
